entry.title: 🟡SAP TechEd 2024 Post Event Q&A with Michael Ameling
entry.content: Join EVP and Chief Product Officer BTP, Michael Ameling, as he answers your questions about all the content and announcements from SAP TechEd 2024.

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
Please note that while we are publishing contest activities on Friday, October 18th; many badge assignments won't be active in the system until Monday, October 21st.  If you happen to complete a contest activity before the badge is active, that's OK. You'll still get credit for it. It just won't show up in your gameboard totals until the new badges are activated and assigned.

If you are looking for Week 1 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 1 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 2 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 2 Contest Activities - SAP Community

If you are looking for Week 3 activities, they can be found here: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities - SAP Community

Welcome to Devtoberfest 2024! We are back again this year with 4 weeks of fun and learning! Starting on September 23rd you can take part in the Devtoberfest contest (CONTEST OFFICIAL RULES HERE) and collect points for tutorials, quizzes and live sessions! Collect as many points as possible until the 25th of October and qualify for prizes such a trip to next year's SAP TechEd in person experience! For general information check out the overview about taking part in the contest here: Devtoberfest Contest - SAP Community Groups 

The table below shows you the points you can earn in the second week of Devtoberfest! Every week we will post an update with the new activities that will get you points in the contest! Each activity will earn you a badge applied to your SAP Community ID and points that you can track on the “for fun only” Devtoberfest Gameboard. The scavenger hunt is also back this year! The tutorials, quizzes and fun activities to earn even more points as part of the scavenger hunt is part of week 4. And don't forget about Fun Friday Activities.  This week's Fun Friday activity will be Spooktoberfest to celebrate Halloween. 

List of activities for contest week 4: 

DAYBADGE CODECONTENTPOINTS
Monday   
Monday#63032EDevtoberfest 2024 - ABAP, Week 4, Oct. 211200
Monday#01171FPrepare the RAP-Based Travel Service200
Monday#605663Generate an SAP Fiori Elements Application based on a RAP-based Service200
Monday#30996ARefine the List Report with Annotations300
Monday#61122ERefine the Object Page with Annotations400
Monday#959595Devtoberfest 2024 - CAP, Week 4, October 211200
Monday#FFDCD3Create a Full-Stack Application with SAP Fiori UI using Joule in SAP Build Code300
Tuesday   
Tuesday#E9ECFDDevtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Week 4, Oct. 221200
Tuesday#716B54Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Watch Video on Process Automation300
Tuesday#BE9EF3Store User Preferences on Device250
Tuesday#0352A0Create Sales Order Business Process450
Tuesday#48A3BCRun the Sales Order Business Process100
Tuesday#A1A3ADCreate SAP Build App to Trigger Workflow250
Tuesday#032218Populate Dropdown with SAP Data (ES5)100
Tuesday#84BBB5Check Status of Process from SAP Build App200
Tuesday#74677BDeploy SAP Build App to SAP BTP100
Wednesday   
Wednesday#FBB496Devtoberfest 2024 - Integration, Week 4, Oct. 231200
Wednesday#B8BE83Set Up API Management from Integration Suite150
Wednesday#0B140FAdd an API Proxy to a Product150
Wednesday#E6EAEBConfigure SAP API Management policies to avoid CORS issues and generate an API Key200
Wednesday#DF57AFProtect Your API Proxy by Adding Application Key Verification200
Wednesday#35857DGet Started with API Revisions150
Wednesday#ACBFDDThe Use Case: Connectivity to Non-SAP Applications using SAP Integration Suite100
Wednesday#102529Preparing Your SAP Integration Suite Tenant250
Wednesday#2F0518Preparing Your Non-SAP Application's Trial Account150
Wednesday#AB9FC6Establish Connection Between Your BambooHR Trial and Open Connectors200
Wednesday#FDBCC7Design an Integration Flow to Connect with Open Connectors300
Wednesday#7B7898Test Your Integration Scenario100
Thursday   
Thursday#5F854CDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Week 4 - Oct. 241200
Thursday#2C210BDevtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Validation Tutorial Jump Start Session on Generative AI Hub - Oct. 24600
Thursday#70C5D3Devtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data),Validation Tutorial Jump Start Session on Joule - Oct. 24600
Friday   
Friday#B9F4FCDevtoberfest 2024 - Frontend, Week 4, Oct. 251200
Friday#1F4654Get Started with an SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Project in SAP Continuous Integration and Delivery600
Friday#D4E4C0Create Automated System Tests for SAP Cloud Application Programming Model Projects600
Fun Friday   
Fun Friday Devtoberfest 2024 - Spooktoberfest1000
Scavenger Hunt   
Scavenger Hunt#9C7B48Scavenger Hunt Completion 600
Scavenger Hunt#011E1DDevtoberfest 2024 - ABAP Scavenger Hunt360
Scavenger Hunt#803668Devtoberfest 2024 - Basic CDS CLI challenge360
Scavenger Hunt#F9F9F9Devtoberfest 2024 - Formula Editor Challenge360
Scavenger Hunt#185D66Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling, Watch Actions Video180
Scavenger Hunt#416F49Design and Deploy Your First Integration Flow480
Scavenger Hunt#548DEEExpose Integration Flow Endpoint as API and Test the Flow200
Scavenger Hunt#2A0305Use the Migration Tooling360
Scavenger Hunt#2F373DUse the Migration Assessment Application220
Scavenger Hunt#000000Enable Migration Assessment Application110
Scavenger Hunt#A1532CExecuting SAP HANA Cloud Tasks from the Command Line220
Scavenger Hunt#BDB308Authenticate to SAP HANA Cloud using X.509180
Scavenger Hunt#2E379AKey User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - Introduction100
Scavenger Hunt#54285FKey User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - UI Changes and Modes180
Scavenger Hunt#D1D1E0Key User Adaptation for SAPUI5 Applications - Custom Actions, Views and Embedded Content180

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 3 Contest Activities
entry.content:
There was an issues with tutorial  #BFAB9F - Devtoberfest 2024 - Use a Local Launch Page
It has been corrected this morning and we are working to assign the missing badges.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @mainak-aich  - We try to avoid that but with different people working on different topics it occasionally happens. It just means that once you complete that one tutorial you'll get double points.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @mainak-aich - yes you receive points for past completions. I checked the system and you have recieved those points.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Missing Badges
entry.content:
@MaximeWalravens 
My report doesn't show DF57AF or BFAB9F as missing for you. Only 2C210B - Which I'll report to IT.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 24th, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/24/sap-developer-news-october-24th-2024/

Description

SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!:

  • Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mE7nHx9XHAY&ab_channel=SAPDevelopers
  • Blog Post by Qui Shi: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-build-apps-integration-with-sap-ai-core-services-part-4-integration-via/ba-p/13885101
  • Official feature documentation: https://help.sap.com/docs/build-apps/service-guide/actions

Devtoberfest Wrap Up

  • Devtoberfest Homepage: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/gh-p/Devtoberfest
  • Devtoberfest Event Calendar: https://community.sap.com/t5/devtoberfest/eb-p/devtoberfest-events?calendar=true
  • Devtoberfest Playlist: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQDHz97qsNTNAE2jnUKj8X5d

SAP TechEd On Tour

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • ASUG Tech Connect 2024: https://events.asug.com/event/9a5f27ca-d742-45bb-85e7-a6597b952900/summary?RefId=sap-splash-page-home&utm_campaign=ATC_24&utm_medium=sap&utm_source=referral&utm_term=sap-splash-page-home
  • SAPinsider EMEA 2024: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024?utm_source=marketing&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=teched&utm_content=sapmarketing
  • SAUG-x 2024: https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024: https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/techx-at-connect?utm_source=TechEd_Webpage&utm_medium=Website&utm_campaign=TECHx_at_Connect
  • DSAG-TechXchange 2025: https://dsag.de/neuigkeiten/sap-teched-goes-dsag-techxchange-english/

ABAPconf 2024

  • Event Site:  https://abapconf.org/abapconf2024/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro
0:10 SAP Build Apps now supports Actions!
1:26 Devtoberfest Wrap Up
3:45 TechEd On Tour
4:49 ABAPConf 2024

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 24th, 2024.

[Shrini] I hope you got a chance to watch this Devtoberfest session we did earlier this week on chatbot development made easy. If you haven't watched it, go ahead and watch the recording as I am using the same project to show you the newly added actions feature that is now available in SAP Build Apps. In the demo, I showed you how to create a data entity to connect to the Generative AI Hub and now with this feature, you could replace the data entity with an action project, configure the input, test, and save the output fields. Remember to add SAP Application Development Actions enabled to true to the destination in order for it to appear in the access project. After you release and publish the access to the library, you can enable it from the integrations tab. Now, there is also a trigger actions function available in the logic canvas and you could trigger this action while providing the BTP destination to get a response from the backend system. The response will now be available within outputs trigger action. Go ahead and try out the new actions feature in SAP Build Apps now, and you could replace a data entity in one of your build apps project with this new actions feature. You could refer to this blog post by Chu Shi, which has step by step instructions.

[Thomas] By the time you're seeing this new segment, our Devtoberfest for 2024 will have probably nearly come to a close. It's hard to imagine but this is the fifth year for Devtoberfest. I know it seems to us like just the other day we were planning our very first Devtoberfest and here we've grown to be this major part of your overall SAP TechEd experience. I'm really enjoying where we're at with Devtoberfest and TechEd. You know with TechEd you get the big announcements; you get introduction to new topics and then you can attend Devtoberfest session sessions to get a deeper dive into those same topics. It also allows us to cover a variety of maybe older topics, but ones that are still relevant to customers and partners out in the SAP ecosystem. So I'm really happy with the state where we are with Devtoberfest. It's great that we had over 60,000 unique people join and attend Devtoberfest sessions. We had tons of people, you know, almost 4,000 people enter the contest. We'll be announcing winners of the Devtoberfest contest soon. If all the validation of winners happens in enough time, we plan to announce those at the ASUG Tech Connect event, one of the SAP TechEd On Tour stops.

Speaking of SAP TechEd On Tour, that's really the next stage of continuing your TechEd journey. journey. If a Tekken on Tour stop is coming to an area near you, I encourage you to sign up and join to get that in-person experience with many of these new announced technologies. If it's not able to come your way, then we have lots of Code Jams planned for you next year, or if one's not coming to an area near you, consider hosting and request one to come to your area. We have plans to expand the parts of the world that we cover with Code Jams next year, hopefully making it to a few locations that we haven't been to in over four or five years. So with that, thank you, developer community, for another successful Devtoberfest, and we look forward to seeing you again in 2025.

[DJ] In this video I'm going to talk about innovating cutting-edge tech from the high seas. He could have kept his anchors away, but he didn't. Right. Tech out. I'll be coming up hot for a minute.

[Rich] Hey folks, Rich Heilman here. I hope everyone is having fun with the last week of Devtoberfest. But today, I wanted to make sure that you all know about the ABAP conference, which will take place on December 5th, 2024. This is a fantastic community-driven event specifically for ABAP developers, which will be streamed online. But you can also enter a drawing for a ticket to one of the on-site venues. The content is delivered in both German and English, depending on the session, and there are a ton of familiar names on the agenda. Way too many to mention here, so head over to the abopconference.org website for more information and details around the agenda.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @vishalakshmi - not sure what you are referring to. That tutorial you show in your screenshot only has four questions.  Not sure which 6th question you are mentioning.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @mainak-aich That is done when you complete all the items in the Scavenger Hunt

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Spooktoberfest 2024
entry.content: Like some others I ask AI for some scary fun themed for SAP

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest Missing Badges
entry.content:
You are missing:
#9C7B48 - Devtoberfest 2024 - Scavenger Hunt Completion ;600

But those have to be awarded manually.  We will do those Monday before we do any final calculations.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: I know we have two badges that seem to be giving us issues.  If IT can't correct them by Monday, then I will just add those points to everyone's account before we do any final calculations.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @Virinchy - That tutorial should be corrected now. Please try again.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
@vishalakshmi Spoketoberfest is just happening today. It will be awarded on Monday after the day's activities are over.  

The Scavenger hunt is manually loaded. It will also be processed on Monday.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
@Benly_Panicker - I don't see that badge is missing on your account. You are only missing these:

#2C210B - Devtoberfest 2024 - MAD (ML, AI & Data), Validation Tutorial Jump Start Session on Generative AI Hub - Oct. 24;600
https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/devtoberfest2024-week4-ai-jump-start-validation-genai.html

Devtoberfest 2024 - Spooktoberfest;1000

#9C7B48 - Devtoberfest 2024 - Scavenger Hunt Completion ;600

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @Benly_Panicker Scavenger Hunt has to be manually assigned. Those will be done on Monday.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content:
@vishalakshmi For the one MAD Validation, there was a typo in the badge naming that was subsequently corrected.  But some people had it assigned before the correction and some after. Therefore I had to put both spellings in the gameboard to grant everyone who got it points for it.  But whichever spelling you don't have will show missing.  But it is in fact being counted under the spelling you actually have assigned. 

As far as Spooktoberfest, I have to ask the team member that pulled that list.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Week 4 Contest Activities
entry.content: @vishalakshmi - We checked your Spooktoberfest entry and you made it after the activity closed.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, October 31st, 2024
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2024/10/31/sap-developer-news-october-31st-2024/

DESCRIPTION

New CodeJam: Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

  • Github repository: https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-cap-llm/tree/main
  • SAP CodeJam topics: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/sap-codejam-topics/ba-p/221407
  • Host a CodeJam: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam-blog-posts/so-you-want-to-host-a-codejam-everything-you-need-to-know/ba-p/221415
  • SAP Cloud SDK for AI: https://github.com/SAP/ai-sdk-js/tree/main

SAP TechEd On Tour

  • SAP TechEd: https://www.sap.com/events/teched.html
  • ASUG Tech Connect 2024: https://events.asug.com/event/9a5f27ca-d742-45bb-85e7-a6597b952900/summary?RefId=sap-splash-page-home&utm_campaign=ATC_24&utm_medium=sap&utm_source=referral&utm_term=sap-splash-page-home
  • SAPinsider EMEA 2024: https://reg.eventmobi.com/emea-2024?utm_source=marketing&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=teched&utm_content=sapmarketing
  • SAUG-x 2024: https://www.saug.com.au/WhatsOn/SAUG-x2024
  • UKISUG Connect 2024: https://www.ukisugconnect.co.uk/techx-at-connect?utm_source=TechEd_Webpage&utm_medium=Website&utm_campaign=TECHx_at_Connect
  • DSAG-TechXchange 2025: https://dsag.de/neuigkeiten/sap-teched-goes-dsag-techxchange-english/

ML/AI in SAP Data Management products

  • Let’s Understand Knowledge Graph in 5 Simple Points: https://medium.com/@raja.gupta20/lets-understand-knowledge-graph-in-5-simple-points-36999c79df8e 
  • Connecting the Facts: SAP HANA Cloud’s Knowledge Graph Engine for Business Context: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/connecting-the-facts-sap-hana-cloud-s-knowledge-graph-engine-for-business/ba-p/13888597
  • Get Hands-On: Build an Intelligent Data Application powered by the SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/get-hands-on-build-an-intelligent-data-application-powered-by-the-sap-hana/ba-p/13916066
  • Accelerate the Budget Approval Process with SAP HANA Cloud vector engine: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/accelerate-the-budget-approval-process-with-sap-hana-cloud-vector-engine/ba-p/13884137
  • Turbocharging Machine Learning on SAP Data with Datasphere, FedML, and NVIDIA: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/turbocharging-machine-learning-on-sap-data-with-datasphere-fedml-and-nvidia/ba-p/13889054
  • SAP and DataRobot Partnership Updates 2024: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-and-datarobot-partnership-updates-2024/ba-p/13916493

The Open Source Way podcast episode: R/3 on Linux

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/release-of-new-podcast-episode-sap-r-3-port-to-linux/ba-p/13918445
  • Podcast episode https://podcast.opensap.info/open-source-way/2024/10/24/sap-r3-port-to-linux/

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 New CodeJam: Expose capabilities of SAP AI Core with the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model

0:55 TechEd On Tour

1:58 ML/AI in SAP Data Management products

3:58 The Open Source Way podcast episode: R/3 on Linux

Transcription

[Intro] This is the SAP Developer News for October 31st, 2024.

[Kevin] If you followed the announcements from SAP TechEd 2024, you know that there is a brand new SAP Cloud SDK for AI for JavaScript, Java, and ABAP. So I thought why not create a brand new code gem to teach you how to utilize AI capabilities within your CAP application using the SAP Cloud SDK for AI JavaScript version. So that code gem is out right now. So go ahead, visit the community.sap.com to see if there is a CodeJam happening right nearby you, or if you want to host one yourself. There is a ton of description on how to do that. And hopefully I'll see you next year in a CodeJam talking about AI and CAP. I can't wait.

[Promo] It's a long way from Philly to Heidelberg, but the show must go on. We're late, aren't we? Yeah, we're late. We all know there's nothing like the real thing. Luckily, we've got our best people on the job. Oh, sorry, Josh. Josh, if you pulled your own weight, we wouldn't have to carry you through this place.

[Witalij] We at SAP are enhancing our cloud native data management products with advanced machine learning and artificial intelligence technologies, providing robust tools for developers and data scientists. First, the SAP HANA Cloud Knowledge Graph Engine announced at SAP TechEd Virtual is essential for developers and data scientists among you working with large language models or LLMs generator AI, as it facilitates the extraction of complex insights from bus data sets. Check Shabana's post to find out more.

Next, with the release of SAP HANA Cloud's Vector Engine, our development team built an insightful Movie Inside demo app to showcase the vector-based querying and storage capabilities. This demo illustrates the Vector Engine's role in enhancing LLM responses with contextual data. The entire demo is now available to you, providing a hands-on experience for developers and tech enthusiasts like you to explore the vector engine's capabilities and applications. To see such a practical implementation, check one of the partner applications known as SBF or Smart Budgeting and Forecasting.

Summarizing long documents and querying using the trivial augmented generation with SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine, this application enables a quicker understanding of the attachments to budget request forms.

In addition, SAP DataSphere, in collaboration with NVIDIA, integrates Federated Machine Learning, or FEDML. This setup allows data scientists to conduct extensive ML experiments on SAP business data while ensuring data security and stellar performance.

Finally, the integration of data robots' AI models into SAP environments empowers developers and data scientists to embed AI-driven insights directly into their workflow. The partnership enhances the technical capabilities of SAP data management tools, making it easier to deploy and manage AI models within existing systems.

[DJ] Did you know that SAP, in particular the open source program office within SAP has a podcast show? It's been running now four years and to celebrate their fourth anniversary, they've published a monumentally epic episode. What is the episode about? Well, in my opinion, it's about one of the few major, major milestones in SAP software history, and that is the port of SAP R/3 to Linux. Now, you might think, oh, it's a big deal, but back then, and this was in the early 1990s, back then it was a massive deal. I was super lucky to be around at the time to get to know the two main actors in this affair, in this amazing event, Christoph Rohland and Harald Kuck. And these two, Christoph and Harald, are guests on this particular anniversary episode of the podcast show to talk about how it came to be, what happened. It's an amazing story. I can't recommend this episode strongly enough. Anyway, get the podcast show into your favourite podcast player, have a listen, and while you're at it, also have a look at this blog post from Ulrika, which talks a little bit more about the show, and also specifically about the milestone that Harold and Christoph will relate to you. So, see you online, and by the way, Happy birthday, the open-source way.

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest Official Rules
entry.content: @T1mey - We have performed the initial drawing, but there is a long and complicated validation process before we can notify anyone or announce the winners.
